meta.title: OASIS Core — ACMA (Merged with MIST 2017)
meta.description: Merged question set aligning the 2017 MIST taxonomy with ISO 55000, grouped into four sections for MVP delivery.

questions.prompt: The organisation has a Vision and Strategy that includes the management of Assets.
The organisation's asset management Vision and Strategy provides adequate information for asset management Objectives and Plans to be prepared.
The organisation has an asset management Policy that is consistent with its Vision and Strategy.
The organisation has communicated the asset management Policy to all internal and external stakeholders.
The organisation's asset management Policy is regularly reviewed.
The organisation has an asset management Strategy that is consistent with its asset management Vision, Policy and Plan.
The organisation has communciated the asset management Strategy and Plan to relevant (internal and external) stakeholders.
The organisation has reviewed and included relevant Technical Standards, Specifications and Legislation in the Asset Management Plan.
The organisation has a review process to assess changes to technical standards, specification and legislation and its potential impact on the Asset Management Plan.
The organisation undertakes regular reviews of the Asset Management Plan to ensure compliance with relevant technical standards, specifications and legislation.
The organisation regularly reviews performance measures to ensure they are consistent with the asset management Strategy.
The organisation has a documented resourcing strategy in accordance with the Asset Management Plan.
The organisation has an established process that reviews and updates the Resourcing Strategy.
The organisation has undertaken risk assessment for all assets and incorporated the outcome into the organisation's risk management strategy.
The organisation has a Asset Information Strategy which is implemented and regularly reviewed.
The Organsation has a Data Strategy to direct the management of asset information
The organisation has data standards to manage existing and new asset data and to guide data development.
The organisation has developed Condition Measurement Standards and included them in the Asset Management Plans.
The organisation undertakes regular reviews of the asset condition standards, assessment procedures and measurements.
The organisation uses costed scenarios to develop (or amend) asset management policies, standards or programmes.

The organisation maintains an Asset Management Plan with clearly defined asset management Strategy and Objectives across the asset lifecycle (creation, acquistion, utilisation, maintenance, enhancement and disposal).
The organisation has a set of standardised procedures for asset management.
The organisation uses standardised procedures to reduce risk and enable confidence in outcomes.
The organisation provides (electronic) access to standardised procedures for all staff.
The organisation's Standard Operating Procedures are regularly reviewed and updated.
The organisation has an established Risk Management Process to identify and control risks associated with all of its assets and services.
The organisation has established technical measures to record condition requirements of different asset types.
The organisation has undertaken risk assessment on all asset groups and implemented relevant changes for continuous improvement.
The organisation has incorporated the outcomes of the risk assessment and control measures into the Asset Management Plan.
The organisation's Risk Management Process and Register is regularly reviewed and updated by staff and Senior Management.
The organisation has provided staff with easy access to all information (including asset inventry, usage, condition and performance).
The organisation collects asset condition data for all assets.
The organisation's Inventory Data Collection and Condition Assessment Process is regularly reviewed by Senior Management.
The organisation has implemented statistical methods for asset condition assessment.
The organisation has statistical data on the overall condition of the assets.
The organisation ensures that work optimisation considers criticality of the asset elements in the performance of the whole asset.
The organisation ensures that asset creation considers criticality of the asset to the operation of the organisation.
The organisation has an established process that reviews and updates the Vision.
The organisation has an established Senior Management Forum for the preparation of asset management Plans and assessment management Practices.
The organisation's asset management Strategic Initiatives and Direction is regularly reviewed by Senior Leadership.
The organisation has identified Critical Assets for its operation and developed a Strategic Asset Management Plan for them.
The organisation has a prioritised asset management Improvement Plan.
The organisation's strategic asset management Plan is regularly reviewed, updated and reported to Senior Management.
The organisation has an asset management Communication Plan to effectively communicate asset management information to (internal and external) stakeholders.
The organisation has documented significant services for which Levels of Service are subject to stakeholder consultation and agreement.
The organisation has an asset management (internal and external) Stakeholder Register which is regularly reviewed and updated.
The organisation has an asset management Communication Plan to manage stakeholder engagement.
The organisation has an established Stakeholder Feedback Process to collate and respond to all queries, concerns and comments.
The organisation's Communication Plan is regularly reviewed and updated.
The organisation has established Levels of Service for significant assets.
The organisation has a Procedure for reviewing, monitoring and reporting on the asset's Level of Service.
The organisation regularly conducts Gap Analysis on all aspects of service delivery .
The organisation's Performance and Levels of Service are regularly reviewed and reported to Management.
The Organisation has a documented procedure for Asset Planning to interface with Master Planning
The organisation has a documented Procedure for future planning and/or growth.
The organisation has utilised future growth in the asset acquisition and planning process.
The organisation has incorporated asset management requirements in future planning process.
The organisation has a process where the Interface Plan (with Master Planning) is regularly reviewed and reported to Management.
The organisation has undertaken stakeholder consultation to establish Level of Service options for significant assets.
The organisation uses stakeholder consultation to drive continuous improvement in its services.
The organisation's Asset Management Plan compliance is regularly reviewed by Senior Leadership.
The organisation has made a future projection of technical targets and methods of measurement.
The organisation has developed and applied a consistent growth (demand) for asset management.
The organisation has undertaken a sensitivity analysis of asset (capital works) programme and demand changes.
The organisation has developed asset lifecycle models and future works programmes including an assessment of the remaining useful lives of assets.
The organisation has a Procedure for evaluating maintenance options against cost and benefit variables (including social, economic, environmental and cultural factors).
The organisation uses predictive models in the development of long-term works programme (4 years or greater).
The organisation's Operations and Mainenance Decision Making Process is regularly reviewed by Senior Leadership.
The organisation's Asset Management System is regularly reviewed by Senior Management.
The organisation has recognised asset management as a core business function.
The organisation has an established process that reviews the Organisational Structure and Culture.
The organisation has a Procedure for reviewing, monitoring and reporting on the Level of Service as measured by asset performance.
The organisation has a Procedure for connecting staff performance to asset performance under their responsibility.
The organisation has an established process that reviews the competence of the staff and the organisation.
The organisation has developed a Contingency Plan as part of the Asset Management Plan.
The organisation has incorporated response time and required resources into the Contingency Plan.
The organisation'scontingency planning process is regularly reviewed by Senior Management.
The organisation has a clear understanding of the data required to manage the performance of assets.
The organisation has a central database of all work undertaken on each asset, including locations of the work when linear asset is involved.
Where separate asset management information systems exist, the organisation is capable of providing consistent asset information across all departments.
The organisation has an established asset management system, with a defined scope for its data management system.
The organsation has identified the data system requirements and implemented a solution.
The organisation has a complete (electronic) asset inventory for all asset types.
The organisation has informed the asset managers on the validity and confidence of the asset data.
The organisation interrogates and analyses activity history when developing future works programme.
The organisation undertakes financial planning for asset management (including allowance for overhead, staff, external fees and future works).
The organisation has prepared a detailed (project level) financial plan for the first three years.
The organisation's financial plan includes projected (future) maintance costs associated with all asset types.
The organisation regularly reviews financial requirements including accrued costs, commitments and future budgets.
The organisation's financial plan includes projected (future) operational costs associated with all asset types.
The organisation has prepared a summary (activity level) financial plan for four to ten years.
The organisation's financial plan includes projected (future) capital (renewal) costs associated with all asset types.
The organisation's long term financial plans are reviewed annually and updated.
The organisation undertakes an assessment of the remaining useful lives of assets when developing lifecycle models.
The organisation interrogates and analyses activity history when assessing asset lifecycle costs.
The organisation has a procedure for evaluating the asset lifecycle costs.
The organisation has prepared lifecycle plans for all asset types.
The organisation uses whole life cost graphs when developing asset lifecycle plans.
The organisation uses (demand based) predictive models in developing long term programmes.
The organisation has a procedure for assessing asset creation options against cost and benefit variables (including social, economic, environmental and cultural factors).
The organisation regularly undertakes Gap Analysis of service delivery to identify missing assets.
The organisation undertakes an assessment of the remaining useful lives of assets when developing preventative maintenance strategies.
The organisation uses predictive models for the development of preventative maintenance programmes.
The organisation has a review process to determine if preventative maintenance is appropriately implemented in the asset management plans.
The organisation uses inventory and performance information in the evaluation of asset performance.
The organisation undertakes an assessment of asset performance when developing lifecycle models and future works programme.
The organisation regularly updates lifecycle plans by comparing asset lifecycle plans with asset modelling.
The organisation's Asset Management Plan indicates the degree of confidence of data reliability of asset performance.
The organisation's Asset Management Plan includes detailed performance targets for the first three years.
The organisation's Asset Management Plan includes (outline) performance targets for the next seven years.
The organisation maintains a future works programme based on a minimum three year forecast of all planned projects.
The organisation prepares and distributes future works programme that covers cross asset programming issues.
The organisation ensures maintenance costs are reported against individual assets.
The organisation's central database inputs maintenance and cost records as part of normal work processes.
The organisation regularly conducts Gap Analysis on all aspects of Maintenance service delivery .
The organisation's resource allocation and plan is reviewed annually and the resource costs included in the financial plan.
The organisation's database has a set protocol for staff access and authority levels.
The organisation undertakes the review of avaliability of products and resources for planned changes.
The organisation's asset performance measures and targets include requirements for monitoring and reporting of actual performance.
The organisation's asset performance measures provide a basis for continuous improvement.
The organisation's staff uses local and external performance measures for managing service delivery and decision making.
The organisation undertakes regular reviews of Asset Health
The organisation's Improvement Programme specifies the timeframe for each improvement measures.
The organisation has an established organisational structure with clearly defined roles, responsibilities and authorities for effective asset management.
The organisation has documented and communicated asset management roles, responsibilities and accountabilities to relevant staff.
The organisation has an established process that reviews and updates the organisational structure and roles and responsibilities.
The organisation recognises that resourcing the Asset Management Plan is a core activity.
The organisation's Asset Management Plan includes gap analysis of current service capability and required service capability to meet future demand and target service levels.
The organisation's detailed resourcing plan is prepared a year in advance.
The organisational structure, roles and responsibilities for long term change management are regularly reviewed and updated by Senior Management
